element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns formation setup and snap mechanics in controlled environment
Player learns to read defensive formations and counter-play in low-stakes matches
Player develops strategic depth with audibles and situational plays
Player manages team over extended series of escalating difficulty opponents

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-based rhythm with natural breaks at quarters and timeouts; momentum swings based on play outcome success/failure

difficulty_curve.early_game: Exhibition matches allow player to learn core mechanics with minimal penalty for poor play
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive opponent difficulty in season play; defensive AI adapts to player tendencies
difficulty_curve.late_game: Playoff and championship matchups feature aggressive defensive schemes and optimal opponent play-calling
notable_spikes: First playoff game introduces significantly improved opponent difficulty
Championship match represents peak difficulty with unpredictable play-calling

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequence of escalating difficulty exhibition matches that allow player practice before season commitment
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Exhibition games vs. progressively stronger CPU teams

notable_level_patterns.description: 16-game regular season followed by playoff bracket; structure mirrors NFL calendar with cumulative record stakes
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Full NFL season with playoff progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Specific play scenarios (two-minute drill, goal-line situations) that isolate tactical mastery requirements
